title: The U.S. World Cup team’s biggest lineup debate has Mass. ties at its core
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Matt Freese and Matt Turner are competing for the U.S. World Cup goalkeeper spot, both with Massachusetts ties.
Freese, a former Harvard player, is favored by U.S. coach Pochettino, though Turner's shot-stopping is noted by experts.
The final warmup game against Germany might indicate Freese's starting position, following Turner's appearance vs. Senegal.

title: New England Aquarium to celebrate World Ocean Day with free waterfront events on Sunday
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The New England Aquarium will host free World Ocean Day events on June 7 at Central Wharf, Boston.
Featured activities include a life-sized inflatable whale, traditional fishing demonstrations, and interactive marine exhibitions.
Free screenings of 'Ocean Dreams' occur at Simons Theatre; free shuttles available from Dorchester and Roxbury.

title: Review & setlist: 5 Seconds of Summer keep the fans at the center of an unforgettable show
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 5 Seconds of Summer performed at TD Garden in Boston on June 5, 2026, as part of their 'Everyone's A Star!' World Tour.
The concert featured a theatrical six-act structure with video segments and a fan-voted surprise song, 'Red Dessert.'
More than 12,000 fans attended, participating in sing-alongs and enjoying humor-infused performances with songs from multiple albums.

title: What 17th-century advice seekers had in common with us
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Historian Mary Beth Norton and Meredith Goldstein will discuss centuries of advice at Boston Public Library on June 30.
Mary Beth Norton's book reveals people sought advice on in-law issues in the 1600s, similar to present day.
The event featuring the 'Tell-A-Booth' and advice history is free, offering insights into human connection and relationships.

title: New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for June 2026
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix adds 'The Big Lebowski', 'Creed', and 'Inside Man' to its streaming roster for June 2026.
'Spider-Noir' with Nicolas Cage as Spider-Man streams on Prime Video; praised for its unique 1930s gangster theme.
HBO Max releases the award-winning romcom 'Pillion', focusing on a sub-dom relationship and directed by A24.

title: Ex-Yankee Sonny Gray earns first victory against New York as member of Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray earned his first win against the Yankees as a Red Sox, leading Boston to a 5-3 victory.
Gray pitched 6.1 innings, allowing eight hits, three earned runs, and two walks, with 56 strikes out of 79 pitches.
Gray, who played for New York in 2017-2018, expressed his dislike for the Yankees after joining the Red Sox.

title: Willson Contreras rides highs and lows of Red Sox win over Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a 2-run homer and clashed with a Gatorade cooler during a Red Sox victory over the Yankees.
Contreras contributed significantly with 2 hits and 3 RBIs, helping the Red Sox gain an early lead against the Yankees.
Interim manager Chad Tracy praised Contreras for his leadership, noting his outstanding work ethic and role on the team.

title: Severe thunderstorms could hit Mass. Saturday evening, forecasters say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Severe thunderstorms with hail, damaging winds, and heavy rain are expected in Massachusetts Saturday afternoon and evening.
The greatest severe weather risk is west and northwest of Interstate 95, but storms could impact much of the state.
Severe weather may occur from 6 PM to 10 PM, with the Boston area at risk between 4 PM and 8 PM.

title: Nantasket Beach stayed quiet despite fears of a social media-fueled ‘takeover’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hull police increased patrols at Nantasket Beach due to social media rumors of a 'takeover' event.
Despite concerns, Nantasket Beach remained quiet with no reported incidents or disturbances on Friday.
Rumors later suggested Boston's Carson Beach as a new location, but no gatherings occurred there either.

title: Red Sox’ Payton Tolle talks ‘playing with joy’ for self, team: ‘I try to come with a smile every day’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Payton Tolle made his MLB debut with the Red Sox against the Pittsburgh Pirates on August 29, 2025.
Tolle's expressive pitching style, marked by strong emotions, is strategic and aims to boost team momentum.
Tolle emphasized playing with joy, influenced by his late mother, and values fans' enjoyment of his games.
